name: Third Class Promenade Gate — E-Deck
description: The iron lattice gate at the base of the E-Deck stairwell that separates the Third Class forward promenade from the main staircase leading to the upper decks — it is locked with a padlock, and on the other side, visible through the lattice, a crowd of steerage passengers has been gathering for forty minutes.
atmosphericDetails: The sound through the gate is multilingual and rising in pitch — Polish, Italian, Finnish, Yiddish, English with a dozen accents — and the faces pressed against the iron lattice are young, most of them, and afraid
The padlock is a standard White Star Line brass lock, and the key ring for this section of the ship hangs in the steward's office one deck up — a fact that is both simple and monstrous
Water is visible at the far end of the Third Class corridor beyond the gate, a thin dark line advancing slowly along the floor — the passengers can see it too
